sessions.intent: Restructure LazyParamsGenerator into OBP shape — extract per-property emission into a polymorphic ActionProperty hierarchy without changing emitted output.

before.assumptions: Source generator stays (no reflection, no runtime compilation). Round 1 targets the 11-way per-property if/else only; markers, ExecuteAsync, helpers stay procedural. LangVersion=latest is already set, so records-with-inheritance work.
before.risk: Byte-for-byte output drift if branch priority or AppendLine ordering changes. Roslyn incremental caching could break if record equality is sloppy. Both mitigated by Phase 0 regression harness (pin .g.cs output before refactor, diff after).

actions.context: Design plan for round 1 of LazyParamsGenerator OBP refactor. Discriminated three alternatives (pre-generated files on disk, runtime Roslyn compilation, reflection-based ActionInvoker) and ruled them all out per Ingi's direction. Settled on extracting per-property emission only, with markers and ExecuteAsync kept procedural. Verified LangVersion=latest on PLang.Generators.csproj before committing to records-with-inheritance.

after.status: design complete, plan approved, awaiting coder handoff
after.health: no code touched, no risk introduced, regression contract (byte-for-byte golden diff) defined upfront
after.notes: Architect role ends here. Ingi approved committing golden snapshot to .bot/. Recommended next bot: coder directly (test-designer optional — golden diff is the test). changes.patch empty since architect-only session.

sessions.intent: Fresh-eyes review of v1; combine structural OBP refactor with contract simplification (helpers move to ActionBase, runtime kind-switch, thin generated ExecuteAsync) so the property hierarchy is built once in its final shape. Replaces v1 byte-for-byte golden harness with a runtime test matrix as the regression contract.

before.assumptions: Directory-as-API is a project-wide convention (Ingi confirmed) — internal generator structure follows it consistently. C# partial classes union base declarations, so generator can declare ActionBase as base while user partial declares interfaces. No existing action handler under App/modules/ inherits from a non-interface base (verified via grep).
before.risk: Bigger PR than v1; byte-for-byte safety is gone in favor of a runtime test matrix that must comprehensively cover ParamKind × {nullability, default, type-class}. Phase 0 matrix design is the load-bearing risk control.

actions.context: v2 supersedes v1. Fresh-eyes review surfaced three concerns: (1) thirteen @this folders looked cosmetic in a non-navigated project — resolved when Ingi confirmed directory-as-API is a project-wide commitment for LLM maintenance; (2) v1 was the second-most-impactful work because round 2's contract simplification would partially dismantle round 1's structure; (3) per-property logic splits across 5+ iteration sites in GenerateActionCode, not 3 as v1 implied. Concerns 2 and 3 argued for combining the rounds. v2 builds the property hierarchy in its final small shape (records carrying IsMatch + BuildResolveCall + optional emission slots), moves all helpers to App/modules/ActionBase/this.cs, introduces ParamKind enum to drive runtime dispatch, and shrinks per-class generated code from ~200 to ~50 lines. Regression contract: runtime test matrix (15–20 minimal action handlers covering every ParamKind × variant), not byte-for-byte golden files.

sessions.intent: Re-review v2 in conversation; supersede with v3 that drops ActionBase, drops ParamKind dispatch, moves resolution onto Data, puts lookup/scaffolding on Action.@this, collapses property hierarchy to two leaves, enforces Data<T>-only handler properties at build time.

before.assumptions: Action.Parameters is List<Data> (verified). Data<T> inherits Data; Data is essentially Data<object> for routing. Data.As<T>(Context) already exists as the typed-view primitive (line 389 of App/Data/this.cs). NeedsResolution flag exists on Data. Variables.ResolveDeep does the recursive walk today, with a discarded-return-value bug on the typed-list branch. llm.query handler uses Data<List<LlmMessage>> with %var% nested inside Content — verified the deep-resolution case is real.
before.risk: Bigger contract change than v2 (which still kept ParamKind). Behavior shift in Data.Value getter (now does work lazily) could affect debugger/audit paths that read .Value for inspection. Cycle risk: Data.Value → Variables.Get → returns Data → reads its Value (mitigated by NeedsResolution=false on already-resolved Variable Data). Migration: any handler with non-Data<T> property must convert (likely small scope based on read.cs and llm/query.cs samples).

actions.context: v3 supersedes v2 after substantive design conversation. Three architectural shifts: (1) ActionBase dropped — helpers belong on Action.@this (the data owner); handlers stay flat with no synthetic base class. (2) Resolution moves to Data.@this — Data.Value lazy getter does the variable lookup and recursive %var% substitution; Variables.ResolveDeep evaporates. (3) Property dispatch dissolves entirely — every Data<T> property emits one uniform line: __action.GetParameter(name, Context).As<T>(Context). Hierarchy collapses from 10 leaves to 2 (DataProperty, ProviderProperty). Build-time validation enforces Data<T>-only properties; runtime trusts the .pr. Regression contract stays: runtime test matrix (~22-25 handlers covering kind × type-shape × resolution combinations). Plan walks llm.query end-to-end as the grounding example — including deep-nested %var% inside List<LlmMessage>.

sessions.intent: Pull runtime2 (Builder V2 + ~50 commits since branch split), assess v3 plan against the new state, and revise. Outcome: v4 supersedes v3 with one architectural sharpening — resolution moves from Data.Value getter to Data.As<T>(context); Data becomes stateless w.r.t. resolution.

before.assumptions: v3 plan structurally approved but not yet implemented. runtime2 had moved forward substantially during the gap — Builder V2 merged plus heavy iteration on Data caching (_resolved flag, _rawValue, ResetResolution, IsDeferredActionTemplate carve-out) and on the generator (per-action callstack/save-restore scaffolding, __SnapshotParams, ParamSnapshot diagnostic).
before.risk: Initial pull surfaced a build break on origin/runtime2 (duplicate finally in generator emission, duplicate Goal.RunAsync/AsData) — Ingi pushed a fix commit (00fd25e3) before I revised. Real risk: v3's choice to put resolution on Data.Value's getter is the same architectural decision the runtime2 patches were plastering over (commit chain f9008255 → 4c22c895 → b0014c91 → fedd44e6). v3 would inherit the same wound.

actions.context: v4 supersedes v3 after pulling runtime2's accumulated work. Walked the recent runtime2 patches around resolution (Data._resolved, _rawValue, ResetResolution, IsDeferredActionTemplate, generator-emitted data.ResetResolution() at every dispatch) and recognized them as a four-step retreat around one bad decision: putting resolution as a side effect on Data.Value's getter. v3 was about to repeat the same decision in different clothes (Phase 2: 'move ResolveDeep into Data.Value lazy getter'). The sharpening: resolution lives in Data.As<T>(context) — fresh walk + substitute + convert per call, returning Data<T>. The handler's __backing field caches the result for the call; ExecuteAsync resets backing fields per call. Data is stateless w.r.t. resolution. This dissolves the entire caching cluster on Data. On the open question (callers that read .Value on parameter Data expecting resolved form), Ingi's stance: 'if it is, then it's not valid implementation. Let it break and find out.' v4 plan: same structural skeleton as v3 (Action.GetParameter, App.Run scaffolding, generator hierarchy) but Phase 2 simplifies dramatically (write As<T>, delete plaster), Phase 3 emits no ResetResolution, Phase 4 grows by EmitSnapshotEntry on ActionProperty, Phase 6 confirms-deletion instead of slim-As<T>. Risks section shrinks (Data.Value side-effect risk and cycle risk are designed out). Matrix grows by 3 cases: DataWrappedActionList (verifies As<T> doesn't walk into Action), ConcurrentHandlers (verifies stateless Data), ReResolveAcrossCalls (verifies backing-field reset gets fresh resolution).
Merge runtime2 into local branch (committed locally, not pushed). Build green after Ingi's 00fd25e3 fix. No code modified beyond the merge itself; v4 design lives only in .bot/.

after.status: v4 plan written, awaiting Ingi's read-back and approval before implementation handoff
after.health: no code touched beyond the merge of origin/runtime2 (no conflicts, build green); design-only session
after.notes: Architect role ends here. Recommended next bot: test-designer to flesh out the matrix (~25-28 handlers with the three new cases), then coder for Phase 1 onwards. Phase 0 review point still recommended after matrix lands.

sessions.intent: Translate v4 plan into a comprehensive C# test suite — matrix handlers under PLang.Tests/Generator/Matrix/ plus contract tests for As<T> resolution, Data.Value raw contract, Action.GetParameter, App.Run scaffolding, generator validation, and __SnapshotParams.

before.assumptions: Per Ingi: no PLang .goal tests (existing PLang tests already exercise variable resolution end-to-end); coder builds the fixture/test helpers; tests encode v4's contract (some will fail before Phase 2 lands — intentional). Existing DataResolutionTests.cs encodes the OPPOSITE contract (snapshot-once .Value semantics) and will be rewritten as part of this plan.
before.risk: Matrix must cover every property kind × type-shape combination — gaps mean undetected drift through the refactor. The architect's enumeration is the starting point; my job is to expand it where the contract demands more coverage (e.g., concurrency, re-resolve across calls, snapshot-on-error).

actions.context: Matrix test classes — one per group, covering every property kind × type-shape combination from the architect's v4 Phase 0 enumeration. Each [Test] body is Assert.Fail('Not implemented'); intent comments above each method ARE the spec. Total ~73 matrix tests across 11 files. The corresponding handler stubs (StringPlain.cs etc.) are NOT created here — they need the source generator to run on PLang.Tests, and the analyzer reference is missing on PLang.Tests.csproj. Coder wires that up + builds handlers + fixture.

Generator-level contract tests asserting v4's source generator simplification: build-time enforcement that every action property is Data<T> or [Provider]; uniform property-emission shape (one shape per parameter property — collapses today's 11-arm dispatch); thin generated ExecuteAsync (no try/catch/finally — that lives in App.Run); per-property __SnapshotParams via ActionProperty.EmitSnapshotEntry. Directly maps to Ingi's mid-stream guidance: 'make source gen simpler.'

App-side contract tests for the v4 architectural pieces: Action.GetParameter (Phase 1), Data.As<T> as resolution entry point (Phase 2), Data.Value as raw read-only (Phase 2 negative-space contract), App.Run scaffolding wrapper (Phase 3). DataValueRawTests directly probes Ingi's big-picture frame: '.Value never does work; Data flows through unchanged; no _resolved/ResetResolution/IsDeferredActionTemplate.' GetParameterTests was originally placed under deep namespace PLang.Tests.App.Goals.Goal.Steps.Step.Actions.Action — caused namespace clash with global using Goal alias (broke SetupTests.cs build). Moved to flat PLang.Tests.App namespace; build green again.

Rewrote in place. The previous 276 lines encoded the OPPOSITE contract (snapshot-once .Value semantics — list/dict snapshots cached on first read, identity preserved across reads, NeedsResolution gating). Phase 2 of v4 deletes _resolved/ResetResolution/IsDeferredActionTemplate, so those tests would all fail. Replacement encodes v4's 'Data flows through, As<T> is fresh per call' integration scenarios: shared parameter Data across action executions, loop iteration freshness, sub-goal isolation, concurrent As<T> safety. 7 test methods (vs. previous ~14) — quality over quantity, each maps to a v4 contract assertion.
Bot output: plan.md (with mid-stream amendment for Ingi's 'Data flows through' big-picture clarification), v1 summary, bot-root summary, verdict pass.

after.status: Test suite complete — 139 test methods across 18 files (17 new + 1 rewritten in place). Build green.
after.health: All test bodies are Assert.Fail('Not implemented'); coder fills bodies as v4 phases land. Tests are designed to flip from failing → passing in phase order (Phase 1 flips GetParameterTests; Phase 2 flips DataValueRawTests + DataAsTResolutionTests + matrix Resolution/*; Phase 3 flips AppRunScaffoldingTests + matrix shape; etc.).
after.notes: Did NOT write matrix handler stubs (need PLang.Generators analyzer reference on PLang.Tests.csproj — coder wires it). Did NOT write fixture/test helpers (architect's plan defers to coder). Mid-stream message from Ingi reframed the goal as 'Data flows through, no unwrapping, source gen simpler' — incorporated into DataValueRawTests, DataAsTResolutionTests fast paths, GeneratorValidationTests uniform-shape assertions. Recommended next bot: coder for Phase 1 (Action.GetParameter) then Phase 2.

sessions.intent: Execute architect's v4 plan + fill in test-designer's 139 stub tests. Sequence: Phase 0 matrix handlers + fixture, Phase 1 Action.GetParameter, Phase 2 Data.As<T> + delete _resolved/ResetResolution cluster + ResolveDeep, Phase 3 App.Run scaffolding + new generator shape, Phase 4 generator hierarchy collapse, Phase 5 [VariableName] sweep + build-time validation, Phase 6 cleanup audit.

before.assumptions: Branch up to date with origin. Test suite (139 methods) all Assert.Fail. PLang.Generators not wired to PLang.Tests.csproj. Migration sweep is small (22 [VariableName] string properties; 250+ already Data<T>). Generator is single 779-line file with 11-arm property dispatch.
before.risk: Phase 2 is the contract change — code that reads .Value expecting resolution will break. Architect's gate: pause if >10 sites need fixing. ResolveDeep deletion may break OnResolveTrace subscribers (need to grep). Phase 3 scaffolding move from generated ExecuteAsync to App.Run requires interface tweak for __SnapshotParams handoff.

actions.context: Phase 0 — wired generator into PLang.Tests via Analyzer reference. ~28 matrix handler stubs in App.modules.matrix.* (parent App namespace resolves cleanly; not auto-discovered by Modules.Discover which walks PLang.dll only). MatrixRunner fixture registers handlers on demand and dispatches via App.Run. Generator emissions prefixed with global::App.* so they resolve in any consumer namespace (PLang.Tests's `global using App = App.@this` was shadowing the App namespace at sub-scope).
Phase 1 — Action.GetParameter(name, context) added. Pure lookup walking Parameters → Defaults → Data.NotFound. OrdinalIgnoreCase to match existing __Resolve patterns. 6 tests filled.

Phase 2 — the contract change. Rewrote Data.As<T>(context) as resolution entry point with substitution-before-fast-paths so T=object doesn't short-circuit. Action-destination carve-out preserves raw %var% in sub-action templates. Data.Value becomes raw read-only; deleted _resolved, _rawValue, NeedsResolution, ResetResolution(), IsDeferredActionTemplate. Variables.ResolveDeep + state + OnResolveTrace deleted. Generator stops emitting data.ResetResolution(). Plain Data.@this resolves via .As<object>(Context) so non-typed Data behaves like Data<object>. ZERO real prod breakage from contract change. 31 contract tests + 15 matrix Resolution tests filled.

Phase 3 — scaffolding (callstack push/pop, save/restore Step/Goal/Event, try/catch/finally with ServiceError translation, frame.SnapshotVariables) moved from generated ExecuteAsync into App.Run. ICodeGenerated gains SnapshotParams() so App.Run's catch can attach per-property diagnostics. Generated ExecuteAsync is now thin: marker init, eager Provider, backing reset, validation, return Run(). Generator stamps __<Name>_set in Data<T> getters so WasAccessed reads correctly. EngineTests.StepRunAsync_ExceptionInHandler updated: handler-exception now translates to ServiceError at App.Run, not StepError at Step. 8 scaffolding tests + 60 matrix tests filled.

Phase 4 — collapsed generator from 711-line LazyParamsGenerator.cs into Discovery/ + Emission/{Action,Property/{Data,Provider,Legacy}} hierarchy. ActionProperty abstract record exposes EmitProperty + EmitSnapshotEntry. Records use string/bool only — no IPropertySymbol leaks (Roslyn incremental safe). Discovery factory dispatches per-property (Provider/Data/Legacy). Snapshot emission moves to per-property. PLang.Tests.csproj enables EmitCompilerGeneratedFiles so structural tests can read .g.cs from disk. 13 GeneratorValidationTests + 8 SnapshotParamsTests filled.

Phase 5 — PLNG001 build-time diagnostic. Discovery emits a Roslyn diagnostic (DiagnosticSeverity.Error) when a partial action property is not Data<T>/plain Data, [Provider], or [VariableName] string. DiagnosticInfo flows through the IIncrementalGenerator pipeline as value-equal record. Manual smoke test confirmed PLNG001 fires on raw scalars. DEFERRED from architect plan: full [VariableName] deletion. Reason: variable.set/list.* handlers need the variable's NAME (not value) — As<T>(Context) flattens variable name into parameter property name. [VariableName] remains the cleanest expression of 'I want the name, not the value.' Logged in claude-md-proposals.md.

Phase 6 — audit + final wrap. Verified no leftover references to deleted APIs (ResolveDeep, ResetResolution, NeedsResolution, IsDeferredActionTemplate, _resolved, _rawValue, OnResolveTrace) anywhere in PLang/ or PlangConsole/. Cleaned up stale RawValue comment. PlangConsole builds. Wrote v1/summary.md and bot-root summary.md.

after.status: All 6 phases of architect v4 landed; [VariableName] removal deferred (documented). 2427/2427 tests passing.
after.health: Generator restructured into a clean hierarchy. Build green, full test suite green. PLNG001 diagnostic enforces v4 contract going forward. No real production breakage from the Data.Value contract change.
after.notes: Recommended next bot: codeanalyzer (review OBP/quality on Discovery + Emission), then auditor. Full [VariableName] removal is its own design pass — needs a way to preserve variable name through As<T>(Context) for variable.set/list.* handlers.

sessions.intent: Review v4 implementation for OBP compliance, simplifications, readability, behavioral fragility, and dead code.

before.assumptions: Coder reports build green and 2427/2427 tests passing. Architect v4 plan delivered except for [VariableName] deletion (deferred). Generator restructured into Discovery+Emission. Resolution moved from Data.Value getter to Data.As<T>(context). App.Run owns scaffolding.
before.risk: Behavioral regressions in As<T> resolution (action-destination carve-out, partial-match fallthrough, IList<object?> vs non-generic IList). Dead emission slots (legacy helpers always emitted). Possible cache-poisoning in IIncrementalGenerator if records leak Roslyn symbols.

actions.context: 5-pass review output: plan.md (approach), result.md (38 findings with file:line), summary.md (this version), verdict.json (fail), root summary.md (cross-version index).

Generator hierarchy review. ActionClassInfo not a record (incremental cache contract broken). Two dead emission slots: __variables, __paramData/ParamData(). __resolutionError dead for v4-shape handlers (transitional). __OriginalDefinition.Name == "@this" branch dead. Synthetic Location.Create instead of using IPropertySymbol.Locations[0].

Runtime review. Data.As<T> recursion lacks cycle detection. WalkList/WalkDict only match generic typed shapes (non-generic IList/IDictionary silently pass). As<T> ignores _type.Convert. App.Run catch deliberately catches OCE (load-bearing for timeout.after, undocumented). SubstitutePrimitive hardcodes Action.@this knowledge into Data — couples Data downward (Rule 1).

after.health: NEEDS WORK — design is sound, but 10 MAJOR findings need cleanup before merge or in immediate follow-up.
after.notes: Top three to fix: (1) ActionClassInfo class→record + List<T>→EquatableArray<T>; (2) delete __variables; (3) delete __paramData and ParamData() accessor. Top three behavioral concerns: (1) cycle detection in AsT_Impl; (2) document SubstitutePrimitive shape contract; (3) comment App.Run OCE-catch dependency. Suggest sending back to coder.

sessions.intent: v2: address codeanalyzer top-3 (record/dead-emission) + critical cycle-detection fix in Data.AsT_Impl + close test gaps Ingi flagged.

before.assumptions: v4 architecture is sound and stays. Phase 5 cleanup of legacy helpers will sweep transitional dead code (no need to touch __resolutionError / dual validation blocks now). Stack-overflow seen in plang test maps to Finding 27 (cycle in AsT_Impl) — fixing that unblocks the suite.
before.risk: ActionClassInfo conversion to record + EquatableArray may surface latent ordering bugs in the IIncrementalGenerator pipeline if any field's hash depended on identity. Mitigated by: (a) value-equal element types already exist (records); (b) new IncrementalCacheTests proves the cache hits. Cycle-detection fix is local — adds a thread-static visited-set.

actions.context: Phase A: cycle detection in AsT_Impl via thread-static visited-set keyed on raw %-containing string. Protects both full-match (variable's value recurse) and partial-match (Resolve returns string still containing %) paths. 4 new cycle tests. Unblocked plang --test from 100% StackOverflowException crash.

Phase B: ActionClassInfo class->record + EquatableArray<T> for value-equal collections (fixes incremental cache, Finding 1). Delete __variables (Finding 11) and __paramData/ParamData() accessor (Finding 12). 9 cache tests + 1 no-dead-emission contract test. Added non-analyzer ProjectReference so tests can compile-time-import generator types.

Phase E (added at Ingi's request mid-session): rewrote sb.AppendLine cascades using C# 11+ raw string literals ($$"""..."""). Output drift: 5 trivial blank-line differences across sampled handler. No semantic changes.

Phase C: documented App.Run's deliberate OCE catch (Finding 33) + behavioral test pinning the contract; documented Data.SubstitutePrimitive's typed-generic-only shape contract (Finding 28) + 2 non-generic ArrayList/Hashtable pass-through tests.

Phase D: trivial cleanup (Findings 2, 3, 6, 9, 21). Most notable: renamed LazyParamsGenerator class to @this per OBP convention; generated folder moves from PLang.Generators.LazyParamsGenerator to PLang.Generators.this; updated 3 test paths.
Housekeeping: gitignore Tests/Modules/Cache/**/%*%/ runtime artifacts created by plang test when %path% directories aren't substituted at file-creation time.
v2 session docs: review summary of codeanalyzer/v1 + Ingi's test-gap concern; plan with 5 phases; final summary with deferred-findings list.

after.status: complete — codeanalyzer top-3 (1, 11, 12) fixed, cycle bug (27) fixed, OCE comment (33) added, non-generic comment (28) added, raw-string refactor (19) done at Ingi's request, trivial cleanup (2, 3, 6, 9, 21) done. 22 of 38 findings deferred (transitional or pre-existing) and listed in v2/summary.md.
after.health: C# 2444/2444 green (was 2427); plang --test runs to completion 165/42/5 (was 100% StackOverflow). Generator output byte-equivalent within 5 blank-line differences. No behavioral regressions across the five phases.
after.notes: Discovered mid-session that Finding 27 was actively crashing plang --test, not theoretical. Phase A unblocked the suite first. Recommend codeanalyzer round 2 for the new contract code (cycle protection, EquatableArray, raw-string emission). The 42 plang --test failures are now visible for the first time (suite couldn't complete pre-Phase A); worth a tester pass to triage which were broken before vs. structural failures the suite is now surfacing for the first time.

sessions.intent: Verify coder v2 response to v1 review across 5 phases (cycle detection, ActionClassInfo record + EquatableArray, dead-emission deletion, OCE/non-generic comments, trivial cleanups, raw-string-literal emission refactor) and confirm new tests honestly close v1 gaps.

before.assumptions: Coder v2 commits 01aa150c..4018f26b address subset of v1 findings: 1, 2, 3, 6, 9, 11, 12, 19, 21, 27, 28, 33. Other 26 findings deferred with logged rationale. Tests: 2444/2444 passing; plang test 165/42/5 (was crashing pre-A).
before.risk: Cycle key (raw %-containing string) could be wrong granularity. EquatableArray<T> wrapper is new code with hand-rolled hash. Raw-string refactor admitted 5 blank-line drift — could mask semantic change. NoDeadEmissionTests is regex-based — false positives/negatives possible. IncrementalCacheTests may not actually drive Roslyn (was the v1 gap).

actions.context: Verification plan for coder v2 across 5 phases (cycle detection, record+EquatableArray, dead-emission deletion, OCE/non-generic comments, raw-string emission). Identified specific verification questions per phase and the test-gap risk.

Verified Phase A cycle detection: thread-static _resolvingValues HashSet keyed on raw %-string with try/finally cleanup. Direct cycles correctly detected. Pass-throughs to ConvertAndWrap return raw string on cycle hit. Verified Phase C OCE comment + non-generic comment placement and content.

Verified Phase B record conversion and EquatableArray<T> implementation (SequenceEqual, GetHashCode walk, default-instance handling). Verified Phase B dead emission removal (__variables, __paramData, ParamData() absent from Emission and from a sampled generated handler). Verified Phase D cleanups (F2/3/6/9/21). Phase E raw-string-literal refactor reads cleaner. Finding 7 (synthetic 1-char diagnostic span) noted as still standing - not in coder's not-taken list.

Critical findings: (1) IncrementalCacheTests is 9 unit-equality tests on records, NOT a CSharpGeneratorDriver-driven cache-hit test as promised. The pipeline cache contract is not exercised. (2) NoDeadEmissionTests's reads<=0 heuristic empirically cannot catch __variables (decl+1 assignment, reads=1) or __paramData (decl+2 LHS+1 internal accessor body, reads=2). Verified via Python regex simulation. The author noted the right heuristic in their own comment but adopted the wrong one. (3) Cycle tests assert only IsNotNull - no value contract. (4) OCE asymmetry test only pins App.Run side; the paired Step.RunAsync test promised in plan was not delivered.

7 v2-specific findings: 2 MAJOR (test toothlessness for IncrementalCacheTests and NoDeadEmissionTests), 3 MINOR (expanding-cycle gap, OCE asymmetry half-fix, weak cycle test assertions), 2 NIT (regex __-prefix restriction, Finding 7 silently dropped). Verdict: NEEDS WORK - production code passes but test-gap concern from v1 not actually closed.

v2 summary, verdict.json (status=fail), root summary updated with v2 entry. Recommended next steps for coder v3: real CSharpGeneratorDriver test, fix NoDeadEmissionTests heuristic + cross-file scan, add Step.RunAsync OCE test, tighten cycle test assertions, optionally harden cycle protection for expanding case, address Finding 7.

after.health: production code: clean. Test code: 2 MAJOR findings on toothless tests. plang.exe and PLang.Tests build cleanly; full TUnit suite 2444/2444 green.
after.notes: Verdict: NEEDS WORK. The fixes for v1 production findings (1, 2, 3, 6, 9, 11, 12, 19, 21, 27, 28, 33) are all correct and verified. plang test no longer crashes (was 100% StackOverflow pre-Phase A). However, the test-gap concern Ingi raised in v1 is not actually closed: IncrementalCacheTests doesn't drive Roslyn (just unit equality on records), and NoDeadEmissionTests cannot catch __variables/__paramData regressions empirically. Recommend coder v3 hardens the regression-prevention layer or Ingi accepts the gap explicitly. Finding 7 (synthetic 1-char diagnostic location) was silently dropped from coder's not-taken list.

sessions.intent: v3: close codeanalyzer v2's 7 findings — fix toothless NoDeadEmissionTests heuristic + add cross-file accessor scan, add real CSharpGeneratorDriver cache-hit test, add Step.RunAsync OCE-propagates symmetry test, strengthen cycle test value assertions, add expanding-cycle depth bound, carry full diagnostic location span.

before.assumptions: v2 production fixes stand (cycle detection, EquatableArray record carrier, dead emission removal, OCE/non-generic comments). v3 work is purely test-hardening + small targeted production fixes (depth bound, DiagnosticInfo widening). 2444/2444 baseline tests pass; new tests should add and not regress.
before.risk: (1) Cross-file accessor scan needs to find callers across 4 source trees — false positives if a sibling test file references the accessor only in a string literal. (2) CSharpGeneratorDriver test needs the analyzer to be reachable as a non-analyzer reference plus ProjectReference; PLang.Tests already has both per v2. (3) Depth bound risks legitimate deep chains tripping it — 32 should be safely above any real case, but verify with the existing AsT_DeepChain_5Levels test. (4) Widening DiagnosticInfo may shift PLNG001 message location in existing snapshot tests — spot check.

actions.context: Phase 1 — Findings #40, #44. Split contract into three orthogonal assertions: in-file dead field with tightened reads heuristic (subtract decl_line_occurrences; =(?!=) to reject == false-positive); cross-file unused public method scan with framework-dispatch exemption list (ExecuteAsync, SnapshotParams); convention pin asserting all generated private fields are __-prefixed. Plus 5 synthetic-source heuristic regression tests pinning detection of __variables-shape, decl-only, == false-positive, and decl-with-inline-init shapes. The test now actually catches the regressions it's named after, independently of whether the live generated tree happens to be clean.

Phase 2 — Finding #39. Added ActionInfoTrackingName/ActionInfoFilteredTrackingName constants on the generator + WithTrackingName(...) calls on the pipeline so the test can index TrackedSteps by name. Added Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp 4.13.0 as a regular PackageReference on PLang.Tests.csproj (was PrivateAssets=all on the generator only). PipelineCache_RerunWithUnchangedSyntax_StepOutputsAreCachedOrUnchanged drives CSharpGeneratorDriver with trackIncrementalGeneratorSteps:true, runs twice with a no-op syntax-tree addition, asserts every output of ActionInfoFiltered is Cached/Unchanged. PipelineCache_ActionClassChanged_StepOutputIsModified is the negative-space sanity check. The 9 existing carrier-equality tests stay.

Phase 3 — Finding #42. Added StepRunAsync_CancellationTokenCancelled_LetsOCEPropagate. Pre-cancels a CTS, pushes it onto context, builds a Step with one Action, calls Step.RunAsync — foreach line 152's ThrowIfCancellationRequested throws OCE, line 157's catch (excluding OCE) lets it propagate. ThrowsExactly<OperationCanceledException> pins the contract.

Phase 4 (Finding #43) + Phase 5 (Finding #41). Strengthened 3 cycle tests with specific value assertions: %a%↔%b% returns "%a%", %x%=%x% returns "%x%", "hello %x%" with x=%x% returns "hello %x%". Added expanding-cycle depth bound (ResolveDepthLimit = 32) in Data.AsT_Impl and AsT_ExpandingCycle_DepthBoundReturnsGracefully test for %a%=X-%b%, %b%=Y-%a% shape (each recursion produces a fresh string the HashSet alone never traps).

Phase 6 — Finding #45 (v1 Finding 7). Widened DiagnosticInfo from (PropertyName, ClassName, FilePath, Line, Character) to (PropertyName, ClassName, FilePath, StartLine, StartCharacter, EndLine, EndCharacter). Discovery captures loc.GetLineSpan().EndLinePosition. Orchestrator reconstructs a real LinePositionSpan instead of synthesizing (line, char+1). Test RawScalarProperty_DiagnosticLocation_UnderlinesIdentifier drives the generator on a partial class with a raw int property; asserts the diagnostic span width > 1. Spent debugging time on a test-only issue: ParseText with no `path` arg makes orchestrator fall back to Location.None — must pass path: "BadHandler.cs" for the test source to receive a real Location.
v3 session docs: review summary of codeanalyzer/v2 (FAIL verdict on test toothlessness); plan with 6 phases (Ingi waived plan/approval — "go for it"); final summary; bot-root summary updated.

after.status: complete — all 7 codeanalyzer v2 findings closed (2 MAJOR, 3 MINOR, 2 NIT). No findings deferred.
after.health: C# 2456/2456 green (was 2444; +12 new tests). plang --test 169 pass / 48 fail / 5 stale (was 165/42/5 in v2). The 6 additional fails are pre-existing infrastructure (signing, identity, fixtures, test reports) — none tied to v3 production changes (cycle depth bound, tracking names, diagnostic location). Spot-checked failing test names; none in the cycle/depth/diagnostic family.
after.notes: Two small production additions: (1) WithTrackingName(...) on the generator pipeline + 2 const tracking-name strings, makes the cache-hit assertion direct. (2) ResolveDepthLimit = 32 on Data — well above any legitimate variable chain (existing AsT_DeepChain_5Levels test still passes). Recommend codeanalyzer round 3 to verify the test toothlessness gap is genuinely closed and the new production additions (tracking names, depth bound, span widening) hold up. Tester pass on plang --test fails is still worthwhile — they were 100% pre-existing as of v2 and remain so in v3.

sessions.intent: Verify coder v3 honestly closes codeanalyzer v2's 7 findings — the toothless tests are now real, the new production deltas (depth bound, tracking names, span widening) hold up.

before.assumptions: Coder v3 commit c9314c5e claims to close all 7 v2 findings. C# tests 2456/2456 green. plang --test 169/48/5 (6 more fails than v2 — claimed all pre-existing infra, none tied to v3 production). 4 test files modified + 3 production files modified + 0 deferred.
before.risk: (1) The new heuristic in NoDeadEmissionTests must arithmetic-correctly catch __variables and __paramData shapes; risk of off-by-one or wrong-pattern that gives illusion of closure. (2) IncrementalCacheTests must drive the actual pipeline, not just exercise records again. (3) Depth bound must increment/decrement under try/finally; off-by-one on the limit, or counter not bounded, would let real cycles slip. (4) DiagnosticInfo widening must produce a span >1 char; the test could pass vacuously if `path` parameter is wrong (orchestrator falls back to Location.None). (5) Step.RunAsync OCE test must drive the right code path (line 152 ThrowIfCancellationRequested, not handler-thrown OCE).

actions.context: v3 review plan: finding-by-finding verification of all 7 v2 findings + 5-pass on the new production deltas (depth bound, tracking names, span widening). Approach is to simulate test arithmetic on regression shapes, trace code paths, and run the deletion test on every new line of production code.

Verified Findings #40/#44. Simulated the new heuristic on __variables shape (reads=2-1-1=0, flagged), __paramData shape (in-file reads=4-2-1=1 not flagged → Pattern B cross-file scan finds ParamData() orphan). The 5 heuristic regression tests pin pattern detection independent of live generated tree. Convention pin (__ prefix) prevents drift. =(?!=) correctly excludes == comparisons. Cross-file scan exemption list (ExecuteAsync, SnapshotParams) is defensive — those have textual callers in App/this.cs. Findings closed honestly.

Verified Finding #39. PipelineCache_RerunWithUnchangedSyntax_StepOutputsAreCachedOrUnchanged drives CSharpGeneratorDriver with trackIncrementalGeneratorSteps:true and inspects TrackedSteps[ActionInfoFilteredTrackingName] for Cached/Unchanged on the second run. Negative-space test (PipelineCache_ActionClassChanged_StepOutputIsModified) catches always-Cached vacuous passes. Generator pipeline now has WithTrackingName calls at the right stages. The MinimalSource bootstrap brings up just enough App namespace skeleton for the predicate to fire. Finding closed honestly.

Verified Findings #41/#42/#43/#45. ResolveDepthLimit=32 + Count>Limit clause closes expanding cycles; isCycleRoot-clears-set on root finally is load-bearing under the early-return path (verified the unwind logic). StepRunAsync_CancellationTokenCancelled traces lines 152→157 of Step/this.cs. 3 cycle tests upgraded from IsNotNull to specific value assertions; expanding-cycle test correctly stays at Contains('%') because the stop string at depth 32 is not predictable. DiagnosticInfo widened with Start/End Line/Character; orchestrator builds real LinePositionSpan; test pins spanWidth>1 with load-bearing path:'BadHandler.cs' arg. All four findings closed honestly.

5-pass on new production deltas. Found one NIT-level dead code: ActionInfoTrackingName (unfiltered) constant + its WithTrackingName call have no test reader. ActionInfoFilteredTrackingName is used by both pipeline tests; the unfiltered one would deletion-test as removable. Documented as Finding 46. Either delete or upgrade into a pre-filter cache assertion. ResolveDepthLimit, DiagnosticInfo widening, and Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp PackageReference all earn their place by deletion test.
v3 review output: result.md (per-finding verification), summary.md (this version), verdict.json (pass), bot-root summary updated with v3 entry.

after.status: review complete — verdict CLEAN. All 7 v2 findings honestly closed, one NIT raised.
after.health: Production code on v3 deltas is clean. Test-gap concern from v1 genuinely closed: NoDeadEmissionTests can now catch __variables and __paramData regressions; IncrementalCacheTests drives the actual pipeline. C# tests 2456/2456 green (locally re-run).
after.notes: Verdict: pass. One NIT (Finding 46): ActionInfoTrackingName (unfiltered) constant + WithTrackingName call earn no test — either delete or upgrade into a pre-filter cache assertion. Recommend tester next pass for the 48 plang --test failures (claimed pre-existing infrastructure). Production code is cleared to ship.

sessions.intent: Validate test quality of coder v3's 7 v2-finding fixes — hunt false greens in toothless-fix tests, verify regression-prevention layer is honest.

before.assumptions: Codeanalyzer v3 verified all 7 closures with PASS verdict + 1 NIT (Finding 46, dead unfiltered tracking name). Coder reports 2456/2456 C# green. PLang test suite has 48 fails claimed pre-existing. My job: independently verify the new tests catch what they claim to catch (deletion test on prod fixes), not just rubber-stamp codeanalyzer's review.
before.risk: The whole v3 cycle is about previously-missed false greens. Highest risk: the new pipeline-cache and dead-emission tests are themselves false greens (a meta-toothlessness). Also: the 6 new PLang test failures since v2 — coder spot-checked them as pre-existing, I should triage independently.

actions.context: Wrote plan covering false-green hunting per v2 finding (#39-#45), coverage analysis, and PLang test triage. Ingi said 'do your thing' — skipping explicit approval, proceeding to test runs.

Performed 4 empirical deletion tests by mutating production code, building, running targeted tests, then reverting. All caught regressions as expected: depth-bound removal → SO; OCE inclusion in catch → assertion fail; 1-char span → assertion fail; mismatched tracking name → ContainsKey fail. Confirms 4 of 7 v2 findings honestly closed.

MAJOR Finding #1: PublicMethodDecl regex restricts to 'public' but the v1 ParamData() regression was 'protected'. Empirically verified against live generated tree via mini console app — Pattern B finds only ExecuteAsync/SnapshotParams (both exempt); misses 'protected static Data()' and 'protected static Error()' helpers. Same toothlessness Ingi flagged in v2 has recurred in v3.
Standard session outputs: coverage data (cobertura + JSON summary), v3 summary.md, verdict.json (status: fail), bot-root summary, test-report.json with 5 findings.

after.status: verdict: needs-fixes — 1 MAJOR (Pattern B regex restricted to public misses protected ParamData-shape regression), 3 MINOR, 1 NIT carry
after.health: C# 2456/2456 green without instrumentation; 2 PipelineCache_* tests flake under --coverage. Production code clean; all v3-added executable lines covered. No new regressions surfaced.
after.notes: 4 of 7 v2 findings empirically verified honest via deletion tests. Finding #40 closure (Pattern B) is the recurrence — same toothlessness Ingi flagged in v2. Recommend coder fix Finding #1 before next reviewer pass; findings 2-5 can be a follow-up.

sessions.intent: v4: address tester v3 findings — fix Pattern B's MAJOR toothlessness (regex restricts to public, original __paramData regression was protected) plus the 4 minor/nit findings.

before.assumptions: Tester verdict on v3 is needs-fixes. The MAJOR finding is that NoGeneratedHandlerExposesUnusedPublicMethod (Pattern B) cannot catch the regression it is named after — the v1 ParamData() was protected, not public. Generator currently emits protected static Data() (4 overloads) and Error() that Pattern B does not even examine. Other 6 v2 findings empirically closed (4 deletion tests verified by tester).
before.risk: Once Pattern B widens to protected, Data and Error become the names checked. They are common substrings — comments and string literals (especially raw-string emission text inside PLang.Generators/Emission/) will false-green the caller scan. Mitigation: strip comments and string literals from LoadAllCallableSources before scanning, plus add 3 synthetic Heuristic_* regression tests for Pattern B that pin IsOrphanMethod independently of the live tree. Risk if the live tree happens to have no caller for the framework Data/Error helpers: fall back to exemption-list with documented why.

actions.context: Phase 1 (Findings #1, #2, #3): widened PublicMethodDecl regex to PublicOrProtectedMethodDecl matching public|protected; extracted IsOrphanMethod(name, sources, exemptions) pure-string helper; added StripCommentsAndStrings; applied stripping in LoadAllCallableSources; renamed live cross-file test to NoGeneratedHandlerExposesUnusedPublicOrProtectedMethod. Added 9 new tests: 3 helper-driven, 3 strip tests, 3 regex assertions. Empirical deletion-test: narrowing regex back to public-only causes the protected-match assertion to fail.

Phase 2 (Finding #4): added Known-limitation comment block documenting --coverage interaction with Roslyn tracked steps. Phase 3 (Finding #5): added PipelineCache_RerunWithUnchangedSyntax_UnfilteredStepOutputsAreCachedOrUnchanged mirroring post-Where test on pre-Where step. Strictly stronger contract — catches transform-step instability that post-Where value-equality hides. Activates the previously-dead ActionInfoTrackingName constant. Empirical deletion-test: swapping the constant for a literal disagreeing string causes the new test to fail with KeyNotFound on ActionInfo.
Session docs: v3 review summary, v4 plan (read back for approval), v4 summary, updated bot-root summary.

after.status: tester v3 needs-fixes responded; all 5 findings closed; no production code modified; both new contracts (regex widening + unfiltered cache-hit) deletion-tested empirically.
after.health: green — 2466/2466 C# tests (was 2456 in v3; +10). PLangWindowForms NETSDK1100 unchanged (Windows-targeting, unrelated). plang --test not re-run; v4 changes confined to 2 test files, so v3 status (169/48/5) should be unchanged.
after.notes: Risk noted in plan (live tree could surface real orphans for Data/Error after widening) empirically resolved: user partials in PLang/App/modules/* contain real Data(...)/Error(...) calls; cross-file scan finds them. Recommend codeanalyzer next, then tester to confirm Finding #1 honestly closed.

sessions.intent: Verify coder v4 closes the 5 findings from tester v3 — hunt for false greens in the new tests, especially the MAJOR toothlessness fix (Pattern B widened to public|protected).

before.assumptions: Coder v4 added 10 tests across NoDeadEmissionTests.cs and IncrementalCacheTests.cs: 3 IsOrphanMethod synthetic, 3 strip tests, 3 regex assertions, 1 unfiltered-step cache-hit test. Plus comment-block caveat for --coverage. No production code changed. Coder claims 2466/2466 green and ran 2 empirical deletion tests in-session.
before.risk: v3 toothlessness was a regex-pattern-matches-its-own-docstring trap. v4's fix could repeat the same shape if (a) the regex assertions don't actually pin protected matching, (b) IsOrphanMethod is pinned but the integration into the live test bypasses it, (c) StripCommentsAndStrings is pinned but LoadAllCallableSources stops calling it without any test failing, (d) the new unfiltered cache-hit test passes vacuously because TrackedSteps is empty rather than because outputs are Cached/Unchanged.

actions.context: Pass 1 inspection of all 10 v4 tests for structural toothlessness; all honest. Pass 2 ran 4 empirical deletion tests. (A) Narrowed regex to public-only → PublicOrProtectedMethodDecl_MatchesProtectedDeclaration failed. (B) Made IsOrphanMethod always return false → Heuristic_OrphanProtectedMethod_IsFlagged plus 3 Strip_* tests failed. (C) Made StripCommentsAndStrings identity → 3 Strip_* tests + Heuristic_OrphanProtectedMethod_IsFlagged failed (the latter because input contains ParamData() in a comment). (D) Swapped WithTrackingName(ActionInfoTrackingName) for literal disagreeing string → unfiltered cache-hit test failed with KeyNotFound on ActionInfo. All mutations reverted; final 2466/2466 green.

Pass 3 secondary toothlessness check: confirmed Data(/Error( have plenty of real callers in user partials (10+ files each) so the widened Pattern B doesn't surface false orphans on the framework-emitted helpers. Live cross-file test passes accordingly.
Session output: plan, summary, verdict (pass), coverage carry-forward note, bot-root summary update, shared test-report (verdict approved, findings cleared).

after.status: approved — all 5 v3 findings honestly closed; 4 deletion tests confirm contracts pinned.
after.health: green — 2466/2466 C# tests; +10 new v4 tests beyond v3 baseline.
after.notes: One observation logged but not filed as finding: integration of StripCommentsAndStrings into LoadAllCallableSources is not directly pinned (Pattern A has the same architecture gap so precedent-aligned). Recommend running security analyst next.

sessions.intent: Red-team the v4 generator-OBP refactor: Data.As<T> resolution path, source-generator emission, App.Run catch filter, JSON ingestion. Goal: pass/fail verdict.

before.assumptions: Tester approved v4 (2466/2466 green). Coder went 4 rounds; codeanalyzer cleared v3. The architectural shift (resolution as read-transformation in Data.As<T>(context) rather than Data.Value side-effect getter) eliminates the previous caching/reset cluster — that's a security simplification, not a regression.
before.risk: Variable resolution recursion is a classic DoS vector (cycle, expanding bomb). Source generator interpolates identifiers — but trust boundary is dev source, not user input.

actions.context: Verified cycle protection in AsT_Impl (HashSet + ResolveDepthLimit=32 + ThreadStatic, leak-free). Confirmed App.Run catch filter excludes NRE/OOM/SOE. Confirmed MaxJsonDepth=128 in UnwrapJsonElement. Action.GetParameter is pure lookup, no side effects.

Source-generator emission uses identifier interpolation but trust boundary is dev source. PLNG001 build-time gate narrows action property surface to Data<T>/[Provider]/[VariableName] — net security improvement. Found __SnapshotParams ignores [Sensitive] (Finding 1, medium, both Data and Legacy emitters).

Standard v1 artefacts. security-report.json carries 4 findings (1 medium info-disclosure on __SnapshotParams + [Sensitive], 3 low: skipInfrastructure default, silent depth-trip, FormatVerboseValue truncation). Verdict pass.

after.health: branch is security-clean; one recurring info-disclosure pattern (medium) flagged for tracking — same shape as the standing Variables.Snapshot() finding
after.notes: Recommended auditor next. If reviewer wants finding 1 fixed first, send to coder — fix is localised (Discovery.BuildProperty plumbing IsSensitive into DataProperty/LegacyProperty records, branch in EmitSnapshotEntry).

sessions.intent: Close security/v1 findings 1 (medium, [Sensitive] not honored in __SnapshotParams) and 3 (low, cycle/depth-trip silently swallow). Defer 2 per Ingi; close 4 — already mitigated by FormatVerboseValue truncation.

before.assumptions: Both fixes are surgical: #1 lives entirely in PLang.Generators (Discovery + Emission/Property/{Data,Legacy}); #3 lives entirely in Data.AsT_Impl. No new abstractions. Tests for #3 already exist — they pin the old swallow behavior and need to be rewritten to assert the new error path.
before.risk: #3 is a behavior change — callers that previously got an unresolved %var% string now get a Data error. Risk of unrelated tests failing because they constructed cyclic variable graphs. Mitigated by running the full 2468-test C# suite. The cycle path's HashSet accounting is delicate (we must not Remove an entry the outer frame owns) — mitigated by restructuring so the cycle return is *before* the try block, only depth-trip is inside try/finally.

actions.context: Finding 1: detect [SensitiveAttribute] in BuildProperty (matched by name to follow ProviderAttribute / VariableNameAttribute convention), thread IsSensitive through both DataProperty and LegacyProperty records, branch in EmitSnapshotEntry to emit masked PrValue/FinalValue ("******") with null-guard so absent .pr literals stay null. Mirrors SensitivePropertyFilter convention used elsewhere in the codebase.

Finding 3: replace silent ConvertAndWrap<T>(strVal, ctx) returns at the cycle/depth branches with @this<T>.FromError(ServiceError(…)) using distinct keys VariableResolutionCycle / ResolveDepthExceeded. Restructured try/finally: cycle returns *before* try (outer frame owns the HashSet entry, must not Remove); depth-trip lives inside try so finally balances the Add. The 'must not stack-overflow' guarantee is preserved — only the return shape changed.

Renamed and rewrote four existing tests that pinned the old swallow-and-pass-through behavior: AsT_CyclicVarReference_ReturnsCycleError, AsT_SelfReferencingVar_ReturnsCycleError, AsT_PartialMatchSelfReference_ReturnsCycleError, AsT_ExpandingCycle_DepthBoundReturnsError. Each asserts result.Success==false plus the corresponding Error.Key (VariableResolutionCycle or ResolveDepthExceeded). Stack-overflow safety is implicit in the 'returned at all' assertion.

New SensitiveSnapshot matrix handler with [Sensitive] on Data<string> ApiKey and a non-sensitive Data<string> Endpoint. Two new tests: SensitiveProperty_MasksPrValueAndFinalValue (asserts the secret is masked AND the plaintext is absent from both fields, while the non-sensitive sibling stays raw); SensitiveProperty_UnaccessedStillMasksPrValue (asserts the null-guard branch — PrValue stays null, no false "******").

Updated 6 DataProperty constructor call sites for the new IsSensitive field. All call sites pass IsSensitive: false — these tests pin equality semantics on the EquatableArray cache, the new field just rides along.
Standard v5 artefacts: review summary of security/v1 + which findings to fix vs defer; full session summary; cross-version index updated.

after.status: findings 1 + 3 closed; 2 deferred (doc-only, low priority); 4 closed (already mitigated by Error.cs:280-296 truncation).
after.health: 2468/2468 C# tests passing (+2 from baseline). Generated source spot-check on SensitiveSnapshot.Action.g.cs confirms masked emission for ApiKey and raw emission for Endpoint.
after.notes: Recommend codeanalyzer next to review the v5 delta. After that, security can re-check that the snapshot masking + cycle/depth error surfacing are honestly in place.

sessions.intent: First auditor pass on runtime2-generator-obp — cross-cutting integrity gate after architect/coder v1–v5, codeanalyzer v3 (CLEAN on coder/v3), tester v4 (APPROVED on coder/v4), security v1 (PASS on coder/v4). Coder/v5 (security #1+#3 fixes) has not been seen by codeanalyzer/tester/security; my pass covers it.

before.assumptions: Trust prior bots' approvals on what they checked. Focus on gaps: cross-file contracts of v5 delta (Sensitive plumbing through Discovery + DataProperty/LegacyProperty + emission; Data.AsT_Impl cycle/depth contract change from swallow-pass-through to ServiceError), foundation ripple (Data.cs resolution path callers), review-quality (do v5 tests pin what they claim?).
before.risk: Behavior change in Data.As<T> from Success=true (raw string) to Success=false (ServiceError) on cycle/depth-trip is a contract shift. Every consumer that accesses .Value without checking .Success is now exposed to null Value where it used to get a string. Generator-emitted property getters may silently drop the error.

actions.context: Read full v5 diff. Cross-checked Data.AsT_Impl cycle/depth restructuring against the HashSet lifecycle: cycle returns FromError before try (outer frame owns entry, correct to not Remove); depth returns FromError inside try (frame Added strVal, finally Removes). isCycleRoot cleanup preserved. Verified Discovery threads IsSensitive through both record types and all callers updated. Spot-checked the generated SensitiveSnapshot.Action.g.cs — masking expressions wire correctly per coder claim.

Cross-file: traced consumers of Data<T> property values. None of the current production handlers check .Success before reading .Value. This led to Finding 1 — the v5 contract change is silent for Data<T> emission while legacy emission surfaces it. Confirmed legacy __Resolve<T> sets __resolutionError + ExecuteAsync short-circuits; Data<T> getter assigns FromError to backing without surfacing.

1 MINOR (cross-file: Data<T> emission buries As<T> ServiceErrors) + 3 NIT (review-gap on the unaccessed-sensitive test, contract-precision on FinalValue mask, architectural attribute name match) + 1 observation (strip integration into LoadAllCallableSources, agree with tester/v4's precedent reasoning).
PASS verdict written. Bot-root summary indexes v1.

after.status: PASS — no critical/major. 1 minor + 3 nit + 1 observation. C# 2468/2468 green (re-run locally).
after.health: Branch is shippable. Coder/v5 honestly closes security #1 and #3 at the security-finding boundary. The cross-file gap (Finding 1) is in the contract claim of error-surfacing — only legacy emission honors it; Data<T> emission swallows the new ServiceError on the backing field. Practical impact small (rare scenarios), forward-looking impact larger (Data<T> is the future emission path).
after.notes: Recommend docs next. If Ingi prioritizes Finding 1, send to coder for the single-line fix per Data<T> emission branch + a Data<T>-emitted-handler test pinning the contract.

sessions.intent: Close auditor/v1 finding #1 (Data<T> emission ServiceError contract gap) plus cheap nits #2 and #3.

before.assumptions: Auditor PASS verdict; finding #1 minor + 3 nits. Property-side capture into __resolutionError is necessary but not sufficient — getter only fires during Run, while the existing line 232 check fires before Run. Need a post-Run check in ExecuteAsync to actually surface the captured error.
before.risk: Adding post-Run check changes the ExecuteAsync return shape for every generated handler. Mitigations: existing tests cover happy path, new contract tests pin the failing path. Low risk because __resolutionError is null on the success path so post-Run check is a no-op.

actions.context: Auditor finding #1 closure. Property getter captures FromError-Data into __resolutionError; ExecuteAsync gains post-Run recheck so resolution failures during Run() surface to caller. Legacy emission already worked via __Resolve<T>/eager validation; Data<T> emission now matches.

Auditor nit #3 closure. Sensitive FinalValue null-guard mirrors the PrValue pattern: distinguishes accessed-and-null from accessed-and-redacted. Data uses Backing?.Value (Backing is Data<T>?); Legacy uses Backing != null (raw scalar) with skip on non-nullable value types to avoid CS0472.

New non-pass-through handler DataWrappedStringUses + 3 tests. Pins the post-Run __resolutionError surfacing — existing pass-through Run() => Body would not catch the bug because the FromError-Data IS the result.
Auditor nit #2 closure (rename misnamed test, add FinalValue null assertion); update GeneratorValidationTests to match the new var __runResult = await Run() form.

Hand-off entry for the next branch: migrate handlers off [VariableName] and raw-primitive partials to Data<T> or successor wrapper. Frames the design problem (literal name vs resolved value), three options with VarRef<T> recommendation, scope (~20 handlers), and what deletes when complete.
v6 plan + v5 review summary + v6 work summary + bot-root cross-version index updated.

after.status: auditor/v1 finding #1 closed (real fix, not just auditor-suggested partial); nits #2 and #3 closed; nit #4 left as documented follow-up. [VariableName] / raw-primitive migration handed off to future branch via todos.md.
after.notes: Auditor scope correction: their suggested property-only fix was dead code on its own — line 232 fires before Run, Data<T> getters fire during Run. Real fix is two-part (capture in getter + post-Run check). Confirmed with Ingi before implementing.

sessions.intent: Documentation gate after auditor PASS — document Data.As<T> resolution contract (cycle/depth ServiceError), source-generator OBP shape (Discovery + Emission/Property), PLNG001 property-kinds gate, [Sensitive] in ParamSnapshot, and apply the two CLAUDE.md proposals.

before.assumptions: Auditor verdict PASS with v6 closing finding #1 + nits #2,#3. Production code stable at 2471/2471 C# green. Two CLAUDE.md proposals on file (test-designer/v1 + coder/v1). No prior docs work on this branch.
before.risk: Architecture docs (Documentation/v0.2/architecture.md, data-generic-design.md, good_to_know.md) describe pre-v4 generator shape with *__Generated records — outdated. Root CLAUDE.md's 'Lazy params: *__Generated' line is now actively misleading. Two ServiceError keys (VariableResolutionCycle, ResolveDepthExceeded) introduced in v5/v6 are undocumented.

actions.context: Architecture-doc updates: expanded architecture.md Source Generator subsection (OBP shape, PLNG001 property-kinds gate, ICodeGenerated default-impl); appended data-generic-design.md resolution-semantics section (cycle/depth ServiceError, dual-capture pattern); appended five cross-cutting entries to good_to_know.md (generator OBP shape + EquatableArray; PLNG001 gate; Data.As<T> contract; [Sensitive] in ParamSnapshot; GetParameter / SnapshotParams).

Root CLAUDE.md: replaced stale '*__Generated' line and 'LazyParamsGenerator.cs' path; expanded Source Generator section with OBP shape + PLNG001 gate + EquatableArray cache stability + test alias-clash convention. Applied both pending CLAUDE.md proposals (test-designer/v1 + coder/v1) by folding into root — no new per-folder CLAUDE.md files created (alternative considered: create PLang/App/CLAUDE.md and PLang.Tests/CLAUDE.md to hold the rules, rejected for tree flatness).

Session bookkeeping. result.md is the CHANGELOG-style entry covering user-visible surface (PLNG001 build error, VariableResolutionCycle / ResolveDepthExceeded ServiceError keys, [Sensitive] masking in Error.Params, ICodeGenerated.SnapshotParams default-impl).

Reviewed website docs — no updates needed. Generator refactor is invisible to PLang developers writing .goal files; the two new ServiceError keys bubble through existing error.handle modifiers without new user-facing API. One minor finding flagged (missing on-error-key example) for tester.

after.health: Build clean (0 errors). All gaps filled, both CLAUDE.md proposals applied. Verdict: PASS — code is ready to merge.
after.notes: v6 dual-capture pattern (Data<T> getter sets __resolutionError + post-Run check in ExecuteAsync) is the most subtle thing on the branch — documented in both data-generic-design.md and good_to_know.md with code examples so future devs touching property emission see both halves are load-bearing. One findings entry flagged-for-tester: missing on-error-key=VariableResolutionCycle PLang example (out of scope for docs; tester decides). Stale 'PLang/Runtime2/' paths in CLAUDE.md left untouched — predates this branch, separate cleanup.
